paris.name: Paris
paris.description: The City of Light, renowned for its art, cuisine, and romantic ambiance.
events: Paris Fashion Week is happening this month with runway shows across the city
The Louvre has a brand new immersive Van Gogh exhibition running until April
The annual Fête de la Musique brings free concerts to every street corner
A new rooftop bar just opened on the Champs-Élysées with stunning Eiffel Tower views
attractions: Eiffel Tower — best visited at sunset for golden hour photos
Seine River cruise — especially magical at night when the bridges are lit up
Montmartre and Sacré-Cœur — the artistic heart of Paris with amazing views
The Marais district — trendy boutiques, galleries, and the best falafel in town
Musée d'Orsay — incredible Impressionist collection, less crowded than the Louvre

hotels.name: Le Ciel Doré
hotels.description: An opulent palace hotel steps from the Champs-Élysées, featuring Michelin-starred dining and a rooftop terrace with Eiffel Tower views.

rooms.description: Elegant 28m² room with Haussmann-style décor, marble bathroom, and city views.
Spacious 52m² suite with separate living area, walk-in closet, and views of the Tuileries Garden.
Lavish 95m² suite with grand piano, private terrace overlooking the Eiffel Tower, and butler service.

hotels.name: Hôtel du Marais Enchanté
hotels.description: A charming boutique hotel nestled in the historic Marais district, blending 17th-century architecture with contemporary design.

rooms.description: Intimate 20m² room with exposed stone walls, antique furnishings, and rain shower.
Bright 30m² room with courtyard views, king bed, and freestanding bathtub.
Duplex 48m² suite with mezzanine bedroom, rooftop terrace, and views over Parisian rooftops.

bali.name: Bali
bali.description: A tropical paradise of lush rice terraces, sacred temples, and world-class surf breaks.
events: The Bali Spirit Festival is on this month — yoga, music, and dance from around the world
Galungan celebrations are coming up, when the whole island is decorated with bamboo poles and offerings
A new underwater sculpture garden just opened off the coast of Pemuteran
The Ubud Writers & Readers Festival is hosting world-class authors this season
attractions: Uluwatu Temple — perched on a dramatic cliff with traditional Kecak fire dance at sunset
Tegallalang Rice Terraces — the iconic cascading green paddies perfect for photos
Sacred Monkey Forest in Ubud — hundreds of playful long-tailed macaques in an ancient temple
Mount Batur sunrise trek — hike up an active volcano and watch the sunrise above the clouds
Seminyak Beach — trendy beach clubs, cocktails, and world-class sunsets

hotels.name: Tirta Sari Resort & Spa
hotels.description: A luxurious cliffside retreat in Uluwatu with infinity pools overlooking the Indian Ocean and a world-class surf break below.

rooms.description: Private 45m² villa surrounded by tropical gardens with outdoor shower and plunge pool.
Stunning 65m² villa perched on the cliff edge with private infinity pool and panoramic ocean views.
Expansive 120m² two-bedroom villa with private butler, heated jacuzzi, and direct beach access via funicular.

hotels.name: Padi Hijau Ubud
hotels.description: An intimate eco-retreat hidden among the rice terraces of Ubud, where traditional Balinese culture meets sustainable luxury.

rooms.description: Serene 25m² room with bamboo furnishings, mosquito net canopy, and private balcony over the rice paddies.
Open-air 40m² suite with outdoor stone bathtub, rainforest canopy views, and handcrafted Balinese furniture.
Unique elevated 55m² bamboo villa perched in the treetops with glass floor panels and private infinity pool.

dubai.name: Dubai
dubai.description: A dazzling metropolis of futuristic skyscrapers, luxury shopping, and golden desert adventures.
events: The Dubai Shopping Festival is on right now with incredible deals across the city's mega-malls
The Dubai Fountain has a brand new choreographed show set to Arabic and international music
Dubai Food Festival is happening — celebrity chef pop-ups and street food markets everywhere
The new Museum of the Future just launched an immersive space exploration exhibit
attractions: Burj Khalifa — the world's tallest building with an observation deck on the 148th floor
Desert safari — dune bashing in 4x4s, camel rides, and dinner under the stars
The Gold Souk in Deira — a dazzling maze of shops with tons of gold jewelry
Palm Jumeirah — the iconic man-made island with luxury resorts and the Atlantis waterpark
Dubai Marina — stunning waterfront promenade with restaurants, yachts, and evening walks

hotels.name: The Sapphire Tower
hotels.description: An iconic 60-story tower on the Palm Jumeirah featuring underwater suites, a private beach, and helicopter transfers.

rooms.description: Modern 35m² room on floors 30-45 with floor-to-ceiling windows and Arabian Gulf views.
Luxurious 70m² suite with wraparound terrace, private jacuzzi, and panoramic views of the Palm Jumeirah.
The ultimate 200m² penthouse spanning the top floor with cinema room, private pool, and 360° views of Dubai's skyline.

hotels.name: Desert Rose Heritage Hotel
hotels.description: A boutique desert retreat in the Al Marmoom conservation area, offering authentic Arabian hospitality with modern comforts.

rooms.description: Arabian-inspired 30m² room with traditional mashrabiya screens, desert dune views, and outdoor terrace.
Luxurious 50m² tent-inspired suite with hand-woven carpets, copper fixtures, and private fire pit.
Secluded 85m² villa with private garden, plunge pool fed by natural spring water, and open-air dining pavilion.

tokyo.name: Tokyo
tokyo.description: A mesmerizing blend of ultra-modern technology, ancient temples, and the world's greatest food scene.
events: Cherry blossom season is approaching — the parks will be covered in stunning pink blooms
teamLab Borderless just reopened in a brand new location with never-before-seen digital art installations
The Sumo Grand Tournament is taking place at the Ryogoku Kokugikan arena this month
A massive anime and manga convention is running all week in Akihabara
attractions: Shibuya Crossing — the world's busiest intersection, an absolute must-see
Senso-ji Temple in Asakusa — Tokyo's oldest temple with a vibrant shopping street leading to it
Tsukiji Outer Market — incredible street food, fresh sushi, and Japanese snacks
Shinjuku Gyoen — a beautiful garden perfect for cherry blossom viewing
Akihabara — electric town, the epicenter of anime, manga, and gaming culture

hotels.name: Hikari Grand Tokyo
hotels.description: A sleek luxury tower in Roppongi with panoramic views of Tokyo Tower and Mount Fuji, blending Japanese minimalism with world-class service.

rooms.description: Minimalist 30m² room with shoji screen accents, deep soaking tub, and floor-to-ceiling Tokyo skyline views.
Elegant 55m² suite with tatami lounge area, private onsen bath, and views of Mount Fuji on clear days.
Magnificent 90m² suite with traditional Japanese art collection, private tea ceremony room, and wrap-around terrace.

hotels.name: Sakura House Asakusa
hotels.description: A cozy traditional ryokan-style guesthouse near Senso-ji Temple, offering an authentic Japanese experience at an affordable price.

rooms.description: Traditional 18m² tatami room with futon bedding, paper lanterns, and garden views.
Serene 24m² room overlooking the inner zen garden with upgraded yukata robes and private wash area.
Spacious 35m² corner suite with both tatami and western seating, private cypress wood bath, and temple views.

santorini.name: Santorini
santorini.description: A stunning volcanic island in the Aegean Sea, famous for its white-washed buildings, blue domes, and breathtaking sunsets.
events: The Santorini Wine Festival is in full swing with tastings of the island's unique volcanic wines
An open-air cinema festival is showing classic Greek and international films against the caldera backdrop
The Ifestia volcano festival features fireworks and a reenactment of the volcanic eruption over the sea
A local ceramics workshop is running special week-long pottery courses for visitors
attractions: Oia sunset — widely considered the most beautiful sunset in the world from the castle ruins
Red Beach — dramatic red volcanic cliffs meeting crystal-clear waters
Ancient Akrotiri — a Bronze Age city preserved under volcanic ash, the 'Greek Pompeii'
Caldera boat tour — sail inside the volcanic crater, swim in hot springs, and visit the active island
Fira to Oia hike — a scenic 10km clifftop trail with jaw-dropping caldera views the entire way

hotels.name: Aetheria Suites
hotels.description: An exclusive cliffside retreat in Oia carved into the caldera, with private plunge pools and the most iconic sunset views on the island.

rooms.description: Unique 32m² suite carved into the volcanic rock with whitewashed walls, arched ceilings, and a private terrace.
Luxurious 48m² suite with private heated plunge pool hanging over the caldera edge and outdoor dining area.
Romantic 70m² two-level villa with private jacuzzi, champagne on arrival, couples massage, and unobstructed sunset views.

hotels.name: Petra Vineyard Hotel
hotels.description: A charming family-run hotel set among the vineyards of Pyrgos village, offering authentic Greek hospitality and wine-tasting experiences.

rooms.description: Cozy 22m² room with stone walls, vineyard views from the balcony, and complimentary local wine.
Spacious 38m² suite with private terrace, outdoor dining table, and panoramic views of the Aegean Sea.
Unique 50m² suite built into a converted wine cellar with vaulted stone ceiling, private wine collection, and garden.

marrakech.name: Marrakech
marrakech.description: An enchanting city of vibrant souks, ornate palaces, fragrant gardens, and timeless North African culture.
events: The Marrakech International Film Festival is bringing celebrities and filmmakers from around the world
The annual Rose Festival in the nearby Dades Valley features parades and rose water distillation
A massive artisan crafts fair is running in the medina with master craftspeople demonstrating their skills
The Gnaoua World Music Festival has just been announced with incredible performances in Jemaa el-Fnaa square
attractions: Jemaa el-Fnaa — the legendary main square with snake charmers, storytellers, and food stalls at night
Majorelle Garden — Yves Saint Laurent's stunning cobalt-blue garden oasis
Bahia Palace — a masterpiece of Moroccan architecture with intricate zellige tilework
The Souks — a labyrinth of markets selling spices, leather, lamps, and carpets
Atlas Mountains day trip — just 90 minutes away with Berber villages and stunning mountain scenery

hotels.name: Riad Zitoune d'Or
hotels.description: An exquisite palace riad in the heart of the medina with a stunning courtyard garden, traditional hammam, and rooftop terrace overlooking the Atlas Mountains.

rooms.description: Beautiful 25m² room with hand-carved cedar ceiling, zellige tile bathroom, and views of the courtyard garden.
Opulent 45m² suite with hand-painted walls, brass fixtures, private terrace, and a sunken marble bathtub.
Magnificent 80m² private riad-within-a-riad with two bedrooms, private plunge pool, and personal butler.

hotels.name: Dar Atlas Garden
hotels.description: A welcoming family-run guesthouse in the Palmerie district with lush gardens, a pool, and warm Moroccan hospitality at excellent value.

rooms.description: Comfortable 18m² room with colorful Berber textiles, tiled floors, and a ceiling fan.
Charming 22m² room with private terrace, garden views, and traditional Moroccan décor.
Spacious 35m² suite with two beds, a sitting area with Moroccan poufs, and a private patio.
